title: Logically Disable A Charger To Reserve It For Manual AGV Boost Charging
summary: Use the Charger Selection view to logically disable a charger so RMS and WCS do not allocate other AGVs to that charging location while a very low-power AGV is manually moved there for a charging boost. The source states this is a logical allocation control and not a hardware power-off.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV is extremely low on power and needs manual charging assistance by being physically moved to a charger that must be reserved from normal AGV allocation.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a hardware charger shutdown.
Do not use this procedure when site-specific physical movement or charger access safety controls are required but not defined in the source.
Do not use this procedure if the charger disable action does not logically prevent AGV allocation as described by the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the charger, verify charger status and associated robot information, disable and later re-enable the charger, and coordinate or perform the manual charging recovery activity described by the source.

safety_notes: Disabling the charger is a logical control only; it does not kill charger hardware.
The procedure involves physically moving an AGV, so additional site-specific safety controls may apply but are not described in the source.
Stop and escalate if physical movement of the AGV or charger access requires safety controls not covered by the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Charger Selection screen
Ability to select a charger by charger ID
Access to the disable and enable charger controls
Physical access to the AGV and charging location

access_required: Access to RMS/OptiSweep Charger Selection view
Permission to use charger disable and enable controls
system_preconditions: A target charger can be identified by unique charger ID.
The AGV requiring assistance is extremely low on power and needs manual charging assistance.

visual_references.description: Charger Selection screen and training explanation showing charger ID selection, charger status, associated robot information, and charger disable use for manual charging.

steps.title: Open Charger Selection and select the target charger
steps.purpose: Identify the specific charging location that will be reserved for manual charging use.
steps.instruction: Open the Charger Selection view and select the desired charger using its unique charger ID.
steps.expected_result: The selected charger record is displayed in Charger Selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended charger is selected and its details are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The charger cannot be identified, selected, or its details are not visible.

actions.label: Select charger by ID
actions.exact_action: Open the Charger Selection view and select the desired charger by charger ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen showing charger ID selection and charger details.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the correct charger cannot be identified by charger ID.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each charging station has a unique ID; clicking the charger ID in Charger Selection shows charger status and what robot is on that charger.
Slide title appears as Charger Selection. The slide and aligned transcript describe selecting a charger by unique charger ID.

steps.title: Verify charger status and associated robot
steps.purpose: Confirm the current charger state before changing allocation behavior.
steps.instruction: Review the selected charger details and verify the charger status, whether it is enabled or disabled, and which robot is currently associated with that charger before making any change.
steps.expected_result: The current charger state and associated robot information are confirmed.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can see the charger status and the robot currently on or associated with the charger.
steps.failure_condition: The charger state or associated robot information is unclear or unavailable.

actions.label: Inspect charger details
actions.exact_action: Verify the charger status and confirm which robot is currently associated with that charger before changing its allocation state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection details showing charger status, enabled/disabled state, and which robot is on the charger.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if charger status or associated robot information cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Selecting a charger displays charger status, whether it is enabled or disabled, and which robot is on the charger.
Viewing charger status, checking whether the charger is enabled or disabled, and seeing which robot is on the charger.

steps.title: Disable the charger logically
steps.purpose: Reserve the charging location from normal AGV allocation while keeping the charger hardware available for manual charging use.
steps.instruction: Use the disable function for the selected charger in Charger Selection.
steps.expected_result: The charger is placed into a disabled state for allocation control.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger shows as disabled in the system.
steps.failure_condition: The disable action is unavailable, does not complete, or does not change the charger state.

actions.label: Disable charger
actions.exact_action: Use the disable function for that charger.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen area showing the disable charger function.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the disable action does not complete or the charger does not show a disabled state.
Do not proceed if the action is being treated as a hardware power-off.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
Disabled charger status means the charger remains physically present but is logically unavailable for allocation.
Disable and enable actions involve callbacks or messages and affect charger reuse.

steps.title: Confirm allocation is blocked for the disabled charger
steps.purpose: Verify that the charger is reserved and will not be assigned to other AGVs during manual charging recovery.
steps.instruction: Confirm the charger is logically disabled so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
steps.expected_result: The charger is reserved from normal AGV allocation.
steps.healthy_condition: The disabled charger is treated as unavailable for AGV routing and queue assignment.
steps.failure_condition: AGVs can still be routed or assigned to the disabled charging location.

actions.label: Confirm logical disable state
actions.exact_action: Confirm the charger is logically disabled so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Disabled charger status in Charger Selection and related charger state information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the charger disable action does not logically prevent allocation as described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
RMS does not route AGVs to a disabled charging location, and the disabled state is tracked in the database.

steps.title: Move the low-power AGV to the reserved charger for boost charging
steps.purpose: Use the reserved charging location for manual recovery of an AGV that cannot be handled through normal charging allocation.
steps.instruction: Physically move the low-power AGV to the disabled charging location for a charging boost, as described by the source.
steps.expected_result: The intended AGV is positioned at the reserved charger for manual charging assistance.
steps.healthy_condition: The low-power AGV is at the disabled charging station and the reserved charger is available for that AGV's recovery use.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be moved safely, the charger cannot be accessed, or site-specific controls are required but not defined.

actions.label: Move AGV to disabled charger
actions.exact_action: Physically move the low-power AGV to the disabled charging location for a charging boost.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training explanation that a disabled charger can be used so the desired AGV can be pushed into that charging station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if physical movement of the AGV or charger access requires site-specific safety controls not described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Manual charging is used when an AGV is extremely low on power; a disabled charger can be used so the desired AGV can be pushed into that charging station.
The source describes disabling a charger so no other robot is sent there, then using that location for a manually positioned AGV that needs a charging boost.

steps.title: Re-enable the charger after manual charging is complete
steps.purpose: Return the charging location to normal service so queued AGVs can use it again.
steps.instruction: After the charging boost is complete and the AGV is removed, re-enable the charging location so RMS and WCS can again assign AGVs in the charging queue to use that charger.
steps.expected_result: The charger is returned to normal allocation and can be reused by the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger is enabled and available again for queued AGVs.
steps.failure_condition: The charger remains unavailable or cannot be returned to normal allocation.

actions.label: Re-enable charger
actions.exact_action: After the charging boost is complete and the AGV is removed, re-enable the charging location so RMS and WCS can again assign AGVs in the charging queue to use that charger.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection context for returning the charger from disabled to enabled state.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the charger cannot be re-enabled or normal charger reuse does not resume.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When you enable it, Geek sends a message saying you've enabled that charger and then we can reuse that station.
After recovery, the charger can be re-enabled so queued AGVs can again be assigned there.

success_criteria: The selected charger is logically disabled and not allocated to other AGVs during the manual charging activity.
The low-power AGV is able to use the reserved charging location for a charging boost.
After recovery, the charger is re-enabled and returned to normal use.
healthy_conditions: The correct charger can be selected by unique charger ID.
Charger Selection shows charger status and associated robot information.
Disabled state prevents RMS from routing AGVs to that charging location.
The charger can be re-enabled for normal queue use after manual charging is complete.
failure_conditions: The charger disable action does not logically prevent allocation as described in the source.
The disable action is misinterpreted as a hardware power-off.
Physical movement of the AGV or charger access requires site-specific safety controls not described in the source.
The charger cannot be re-enabled for normal reuse after the manual charging activity.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the charger disable action does not logically prevent AGV allocation.
Escalate if safe physical movement of the AGV cannot be performed under documented site controls.
Escalate if charger state cannot be confirmed in Charger Selection.
Escalate if the charger cannot be returned to enabled status after use.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen, charger ID selection, charger status, associated robot, and disable/enable charger context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.
Manual charging is used when an AGV is extremely low on power; a disabled charger can be used so the desired AGV can be pushed into that charging station.
When you enable it, Geek sends a message saying you've enabled that charger and then we can reuse that station.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact button labels or confirmation prompts for disable or enable actions.
The source does not define detailed physical safety controls for manually moving the AGV.
The source does not provide a formal verification method beyond the stated logical behavior that RMS will not route AGVs to the disabled charger.
The source does not provide a time estimate for the procedure.
The source does not specify whether additional personnel are required for physical AGV movement.
